title: Shared Parental Leave Benefit (SPB)
text:
Shared Parental Leave Benefit (SPB) - SupportGoWhere
Shared Parental Leave Benefit (SPB)
Share link
Support eligible working parents who do not qualify for the Shared Parental Leave (SPL) scheme due to their employment arrangements.
SCHEME HIGHLIGHTS
For natural or adoptive parents who do not qualify for Shared Parental Leave (SPL) due to their employment arrangement.
Eligible parents can receive Shared Parental Leave Benefit (SPB), capped at $2,500 per week.
Apply now
Collapse all sections
About this support

For child born/estimated delivery date/Formal Intent to Adopt (FIA) date between 1 Apr 2025 to 31 Mar 2026, eligible parents can claim up to 6 weeks of income support. 

For child born/estimated delivery date/FIA date on or after 1 Apr 2026, eligible parents can claim up to 10 weeks of income support.  

Government payment capped at $2,500 per week. 

Who is this for?

For natural parents with live births: 

Child’s birth or estimated delivery date: 

If between 1 Apr 2025 to 31 Mar 2026, entitled to 6 weeks

If on or after 1 Apr 2026, entitled to 10 weeks  

Your child is a Singapore citizen or becomes one within 12 months from birth 




For adoptive parents: 

You are not the natural father or mother of the child 

Child’s Formal Intent to Adopt (FIA) date: 

If between 1 Apr 2025 to 31 Mar 2026, entitled to 6 weeks  

If on or after 1 Apr 2026, entitled to 10 weeks  

The child must be below 12 months old on FIA date 

If child is not a Singapore Citizen (SC), either adoptive mother or father of child has been SC since the child’s dependant’s pass issuance 




Applicable to all: 

Employed or self-employed for at least 90 days in the 12 months before child’s birth 

For fathers: lawfully married to the child’s mother between conception and birth or within 12 months of birth 

Not eligible for Shared Parental Leave (SPL) except under specific conditions such as end of employment contract or retrenchment 

How to apply?

Please refer to details on the GPL Portal.

Where can I find help?

View more details 

MSFCares: 1800 111 2222




(say ‘GPLS’ when asked for the hotline you want to reach)

Airtime charges apply for mobile calls to 1800 service lines. Calls are free of charge only if made from regular land lines in Singapore.

Submit an enquiry

Scheme last updated 15 Dec 2025
SUPPORT RECOMMENDER
Answer a few questions to find schemes and services you may be eligible for.
Check now
